trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline stage complete. Reference PyTorch: 1.97ms, Triton kernel: 0.624ms (3.16x speedup, score 81.5). Profiling shows 100% time in depthwise_conv2d_kernel. Advancing to optimization stage to push further.

nudges.text: You've spent significant time debugging a Triton kernel without a baseline. Consider first creating a simple `ModelNew` that just delegates to `torch.nn.functional.conv2d` with `groups=in_channels` (i.e., wrapping the reference implementation) to establish a working baseline score. Once you have a passing correctness check and a baseline number, you can then replace the forward with an optimized Triton kernel. This also avoids the bias/device issues since you won't need a dummy bias tensor.

The test harness creates both models with the same random seed so they get identical weight initialization. Instead of manually creating `nn.Parameter` and trying to match initialization, just use `self.conv = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, (kernel_size_h, kernel_size_w), stride=(...), padding=(...), dilation=(...), groups=groups, bias=bias)` internally, then in `forward()` extract `self.conv.weight` and pass it to your Triton kernel. This guarantees identical weights.
